task_objective: Implement the five Codex GPT-5 review-hardening plans for BrowserView normalization, conflict metadata timestamps, process tree traversal, _run_blocking cancellation cleanup, and unload stop offloading.
design_decisions: Used the implementer skill workflow and preserved the repository protocol lifecycle with baseline, red, green, refactor, validation, and documentation steps.
Replaced BrowserView in-place alias mutation with an adapter that delegates to native uppercase or lowercase methods without mutating frozen Steam Client objects.
Kept nested BrowserView destruction available while omitting adapter Destroy when the raw view is the owner, preventing owner double-destruction.
Delegated SetTopmost through the BrowserView adapter so existing topmost behavior is preserved when the native API exposes it.
Stored local conflict metadata timestamps as timezone-aware UTC ISO strings and converted valid conflict timestamps to the user's local timezone in the frontend.
Converted process tree traversal from recursive DFS to iterative DFS with a visited set to avoid recursion failures and cycle loops.
Guarded late _run_blocking reader callbacks from reading or closing a reused file descriptor after cancellation closes the original read side.
Moved unload stop through the existing blocking-call helper and added a synchronous fallback if offloading fails before backend.stop runs.
Fixed the local TDD hook's unset test_file variable so it can validate existing tests for modified backend modules.
results: Baseline full pytest passed before implementation: 261 passed.
New tests failed before implementation in the targeted red run.
Targeted issue suites passed after implementation: 170 passed.
Validated TypeScript with ./run.sh pnpm run typecheck.

Validated the full Python suite with ./run.sh uv run pytest: 269 passed.
